questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> does not exist because its bond order is zero.
Any two atomic orbitals can combine to form two molecular orbitals.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></msub><mo>)</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></msub><mo>)</mo></math>are degenerate molecular orbitals
questions.solution: Only those atomic orbitals, that have equal or nearly equal energy combine to form molecular orbitals. This is because these orbitals have zero or less energy gap between them, which helps to provide them better overlapping. Hence, they form stable bonds.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: What is the hybridization and geometry of the compound <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>XeOF</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo> </mo><mo>?</mo></math>
questions.solution:
The structure of XeOF4 is.

So in this sigma bond =5

Pi bonds = 1,

lone pairs = 1

So total hybrid orbital requires = 5+1=6, (pi bond do not need hybrid orbitals),

Hence, the hybridisation will be sp3d2. As it has one lone pair and five bond pairs, so according to VSEPR, the geometry will be square pyramidal,
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following has highest second ionization energy?
questions.solution:
Electronic configuration for chromium is :
$$
{ }_{24} \mathrm{Cr} \rightarrow 1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^6 3 s^2 3 p^6 3 d^5 4 s^1
$$
After ionisation of $4 s$ electron the next electron which will be ionised is from $3 d$ orbital. As $3 d^5$ electronic configuration, is stable, hence, the second ionisation of $\mathrm{Cr}$ will require much greater ionisation energy than the expected one.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Lattice energy of an ionic compound depends upon :
questions.solution:
Both
Lattice Energy∝q1q2rc+ra

Lattice energy is defined as the heat of formation for ions of opposite charges in the gas phase to combine into an ionic solid.it is directly proportional to charge on ions and inversely proportional to size of ions.

questions.q: In $\mathrm{PO}_{4}^{3-},$ the formal charge on each oxygen atom and the $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{O}$ bond order respectively are
questions.solution:
Bond order
$=\frac{\text { Number of bonds }}{\text { Number of Resonating structures }}$
$=\frac{5}{4}=1.25$
Three unit negative charge is being shared by four $\mathrm{O}$ atoms. Formal charge $=-3 / 4$

questions.q: The charge/size ratio of a cation determines its polarizing power. Which one of the following sequences represents the increasing order of the polarizinig order of the polarizing power of the cationic species, $\mathrm{K}^{+}, \mathrm{Ca}^{2+}, \mathrm{Mg}^{2+}, \mathrm{Be}^{2+}$ ?
questions.solution:
Higher the charge/size ratio, more is the polarizing power.
$\therefore \mathrm{K}^{+} < \mathrm{Ca}^{2+} < \mathrm{Mg}^{2+} < \mathrm{Be}^{2+}$
Hence, (C) is correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: The $\mathrm{BCl}_3$ is a planar molecule where as $\mathrm{NCl}_3$ is pyramidal, because
options: $\mathrm{B}-\mathrm{Cl}$ bond is more polar than $\mathrm{N}-\mathrm{Cl}$ bond
$\mathrm{N}-\mathrm{Cl}$ bond is more covalent than $\mathrm{B}-\mathrm{Cl}$ bond
nitrogen atom is smaller than boron atoms
$\mathrm{BCl}_3$ has no lone pair but $\mathrm{NCl}_3$ has a lone pair of electrons.
questions.solution:
No lone pair of electron is available in $\mathrm{BCl}_3$.
One lone pair of electron is available on N-atoms, it occupies a corner in the tetrahedral arrangement. Therefore, $\mathrm{NCl}_3$ appears pyramidal in shape.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Assuming the Hund's rule violated, the bond order and magnetic nature of the diatomic molecule of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is:
questions.solution:
According to the Hund's rule of maximum multiplicity, if two or more orbitals of equal energy are available, the electrons will occupy them singly before filling them in pairs.

B2=(σ1s2)(σ1s2*)(σ2s2)(σ2s2*)(π2px2)

Number of bonding electrons=6 

Number of anti-bonding electrons=4

Bond order=Number of bonding electrons−Number of anti‐bonding electrons​2=6−4​2=1

Since there are no unpaired electrons, it is diamagnetic in nature.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

The possible shapes of molecules may be (consider C has no Lone pair) (A, B and C are are more electronegative than X)

questions.solution:
The bond length follows the order

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion : $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{OCH}_3$ and $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{OH}$ has comparable molecular weight but boiling point of $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{OH}$ is more than dimethyl ether.
Reason : $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{OH}$ forms intermolecular Hbonding while $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{OCH}_3$ forms intramolecular $\mathrm{H}$-bonding.
options: If both Assertion and Reason are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
If both Assertion and Reason are true but Reason is not the correct explanation of Assertion.
If Assertion is true but Reason is false.
If Assertion is false but Reason is true.
questions.solution: Due to the presence of hydroxyl group $(-\mathrm{OH})$, there is extensive hydrogen bonding between the ethanol molecules $\left(\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{OH}\right)$. But there is no such hydrogen bonding in dimethyl ether (due to absence of -OH group). So boiling point of dimethyl ether is much lower than ethanol.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q:
After understanding the assertion and reason, choose the correct option.

Assertion: In the bonding molecular orbital (MO) of H 2 , electron density is increased between the nuclei.
Reason: The bonding MO is ψA+ψB , which shows destructive interference of the combining electron waves.
options: Assertion and Reason are correct, but Reason is not the correct explanation for the Assertion.
Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation for the Assertion.
Assertion is incorrect, Reason is correct.
Assertion is correct, Reason is incorrect.
questions.solution:
Electron density between nuclei increased during formation of BMO in H2 .



BMO is ψA+ψB (Linear combination of Atomtextc orbitals) provides constructive interference.
While by distructive interference anti BMO ψ A − ψ B formed.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The bond length of $\mathrm{HCl}$ molecule is $1.275 Å$ and its dipole moment is $1.03 \mathrm{D}$. The ionic character of the molecule (in percent) (charge of the electron $=4.8 \times 10^{-10} \mathrm{esu}$ ) is
questions.solution:
Given,
observed dipole moment $=1.03 \mathrm{D}$
Bond length of $\mathrm{HCl}$ molecule, $d=1.275 Å$
$$
=1.275 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{~cm}
$$
Charge of electron, $\quad e^{-}=4.8 \times 10^{-10}$ esu
Percentage ionic character $=$ ?
Theoretical value of dipole moment $=e \times d$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =4.8 \times 10^{-10} \times 1.275 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{esu}-\mathrm{cm} \\
& =6.12 \times 10^{-18} \mathrm{esu}-\mathrm{cm} \\
& =6.12 \mathrm{D}
\end{aligned}
$$
Percentage ionic character
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{\text { observed dipole moment }}{\text { theoretical value of dipole moment }} \times 100 \\
& =\frac{1.03}{6.12} \times 100 \\
& =16.83 \%
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
